Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Understand the Goal The objective is to create an accepting Petri net using pm4py based on a specific insurance claim management process The net needs an initial marking im and a final marking fm

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Analyze the Process Start Customer files an Insurance Claim C Step 1 Claim Registration CR Step 2 Document Verification DV Loop 1 If documents incomplete then Re Document Submission RDS then back to DV Can happen multiple times Step 3 Fraud Check FC Loop 2 If doubts raised then In depth Investigation II then back before proceeding to Claim Assessment CA Can happen multiple times Step 4 Claim Assessment CA Step 5 Approval AP Step 6 Payment P Step 7 Notification N Step 8 Closure CL End Claim closed

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Map to Petri Net Elements Places States p start New claim arriving p1 Ready for CR p2 CR done ready for DV p3 DV done ready for RDS loop or FC p4 FC done ready for II loop or CA p5 ready for CA p6 CA done ready for AP p7 AP done ready for P p8 P done ready for N p9 N done ready for CL p end CL done claim closed Transitions Activities t C Insurance Claim t CR Claim Registration t DV Document Verification t RDS Re Document Submission t FC Fraud Check t II In depth Investigation t CA Claim Assessment t AP Approval t P Payment t N Notification t CL Closure

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Refine Place Transition Logic Wait lets look at the loop structure So p3 after DV has two outgoing arcs one to t RDS one to t FC p4 after FC has two outgoing arcs one to t II one to t CA Where does t II go Back to p3 before FC or back to p4 If it goes back to p4 it loops after FC Usually investigation is done and then you do FC again Lets make t II go to p3 State ready for Fraud Check

Name: Validation - PE
Text: This perfectly models multiple II FC iterations before CA Loop for RDS p2 to t DV to p3 p3 to t RDS to p2 p3 to t FC to p4 This perfectly models multiple DV RDS iterations before FC

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Drafting the Code Imports from pm4py objects petri net obj import PetriNet Marking from pm4py objects petri net utils import petri utils Net creation net equals PetriNet name insurance claim process Places p start p1 ready CR p2 ready DV p3 ready FC or RDS p4 ready CA or II p5 ready AP p6 ready P p7 ready N p8 ready CL p end Transitions t C Insurance Claim t CR Claim Registration t DV Document Verification t RDS Re Document Submission t FC Fraud Check t II In depth Investigation t CA Claim Assessment t AP Approval t P Payment t N Notification t CL Closure Adding to net places and transitions Arcs Main flow p start to t C to p1 p1 to t CR to p2 p2 to t DV to p3 p3 to t FC to p4 p4 to t CA to p5 p5 to t AP to p6 p6 to t P to p7 p7 to t N to p8 p8 to t CL to p end Loops p3 to t RDS to p2 and p4 to t II to p3 Markings im token in p start fm token in p end

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Self Correction on Loop 2 Is it better for t II to go back to p3 ready for FC or just loop to itself Going back to p3 makes sense because the prompt says if the Fraud Check FC raises doubts an additional In depth Investigation II is performed before proceeding to Claim Assessment CA This implies a cycle of FC II FC II then CA So t II to p3 to t FC is highly accurate Lets keep it as t II to p3

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Review against constraints accepting Petri net Yes defined net im fm places representing states between these activities Yes transitions labeled with the activity names Yes arcs connecting them to form a logical sequence with possible loops Yes between DV and RDS if documents need re submission Yes between FC and II if in depth investigation is required multiple times Yes initial marking should place a token in a start place Yes final marking should represent a closed claim Yes

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Double check the sequence C Insurance Claim CR Claim Registration DV Document Verification RDS Re Document Submission loops to DV FC Fraud Check II In depth Investigation loops to FC CA Claim Assessment AP Approval P Payment N Notification CL Closure Matches the prompt exactly The structure is solid
